planets.name: Feel

artifacts.title: Feel receipt from 2024-01-13
artifacts.excerpt:
a friend who reads a lot offhandedly said

"yeah I liked the book! but it has some 'first novel syndrome' - the author is trying to make this book contain the entire world"

I think about this often
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: feel, love, something, too.

artifacts.title: Feel receipt from 2024-04-19
artifacts.excerpt:
“self love” discourse is fine and great but I think the main thing it misses 

is a real, deep soul need for attachments to trustworthy & relationally-skilled friends and *elders*.

even with all the self-love in the world: without wise teachers we are unnecessarily fumbling https://t.co/yJ3OhAXZZt
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: feel, love, something, too.

artifacts.title: Feel receipt from 2023-12-03
artifacts.excerpt:
one thing I honestly do not understand is why there is not more serious scientific inquiry about “what happens after we die”

except for this one UVa prof with a database of 2.5k cases of kids who report memories of past lives 

some of which are pretty unignorable https://t.co/GfkIFmigPq
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: feel, love, something, too.

artifacts.title: Feel receipt from 2024-04-26
artifacts.excerpt:
woah woah. my mythosomatics teacher (Josh Schrei) expects that in 10-20 years, we’ll see a cultural shift *away* from tech and phones, simply because the present state is “not somatically tenable”

or it’ll be similar to ‘fire’ which lives in ‘fireplaces’ and not elsewhere
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: feel, love, something, too.

artifacts.title: Here receipt from 2023-10-10
artifacts.excerpt:
my main observation of transhumanists - now that I’m back in the Bay Area 

is that they seem to be people that aren’t enjoying “being human” very much. 

imo - let’s get “how to enjoy life here” sorted, before talking about transcending it. it’s possible. order of operations
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: here, now, life, something.

artifacts.title: Here receipt from 2023-11-20
artifacts.excerpt:
I’ve been struggling to integrate my hyperlogical side with my hyperartistic side

usually people who see one, can’t value the other

so I put them in one place, on the same sheet of paper!

here’s (new) drawings on top of my notes from a general relativity class (years ago) https://t.co/jzxuNofqgz
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: here, now, life, something.

planets.name: Life

artifacts.title: Life receipt from 2023-05-19
artifacts.excerpt:
one thing I find weird about the norm of meeting up with friends only to hang out / have fun is -

“wait, so am I supposed to do all the *work* of living life entirely on my own”?

I so want more meeting up to bulk-cook or cowork or support general life maintenance
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: life, love, now, feels.

artifacts.title: Life receipt from 2024-04-12
artifacts.excerpt:
starting to think "am I caring and being cared for" might be a great barometer for general quality-of-life.

quoth Robin Wall Kimmerer "It is a terrible punishment to be banished from the web of reciprocity, with no one to share with you and no one for you to care for"
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: life, love, now, feels.

artifacts.title: Life receipt from 2023-08-17
artifacts.excerpt:
very subtle but important difference between doing inner work because

“fuck, my life has problems, I inherited gifts but also a mess, I want things to be better for me and my loved ones”

and

“fuck, I’m hopelessly broken, I need to fix myself to become loveable”

do the first
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: life, love, now, feels.

artifacts.title: Now receipt from 2024-04-07
artifacts.excerpt:
twitter is a wild and wonderful place.

I now have funding AND a collaborator towards "researching the quantum-physical chemistry of possible ways microwaves might have weird effects on food"

I will blog about it. wanna donate to take the project further? gofundme link below 💜
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: now, right, massage, time.

artifacts.title: Now receipt from 2024-08-13
artifacts.excerpt:
holy heck

the evidence is stacking up - the Indian medical system really, truly, can perform acts of healing that the US medical system cannot.

I'm surprised at the lack of "collaboration" or "learning from them" that I've seen up till now https://t.co/7X9o1ZGKoq https://t.co/5YHL9rZ7VL
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: now, right, massage, time.

artifacts.title: Now receipt from 2024-08-15
artifacts.excerpt:
as a mathematician, I thought my subtle interoceptive bodily sensations were noise. 

a slight pull in my right side neck, a twitch in my arm when moved to a particular angle - they “just happened sometimes.”

now as a bodyworker, I recognize they contain a whole lot of signal https://t.co/EAKdf243mw
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: now, right, massage, time.

artifacts.title: Now receipt from 2023-01-23
artifacts.excerpt: Everything that once could be addressed communally and cosmically through the ritual enactment of ecstasy, addressed in a festival of enacted mourning, addressed with candleflame and sprinkled water, is now somehow up to the individual to work out within their own heads.
